creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 4/23/12 1:29 PM, jimmey prestwood wrote:
> Hey Michael,
>
>
> Your splitting hairs with words when you state "kde4 actually does not compile
> anything "there is a Makefile in the directory which implies when you type make
> install clean in this directory that something will be compiled whether it is
> Kde4 or sub ports that make up Kde4.
>
> Now, with that said, the sub-port causing this predicament for Kde4 is libxine,
> which was already stated. I have update the ports tree twice to no avail!
>
> Thanks, for your insights into this matter!
the hairs you split are you own.  you are asking for help, and the more 
information you give, the better chances someone can help you.

like I said, ask in the freebsd-ports@ email list.

out of the box, without anything left over from any previous compiles, 
libxine SEEMS to compile just fine, and doesn't seem to try to link libxcb.

<http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/logs/9-scheidell_I386/libxine-1.2.1.log>

this is a 9.0 release, I386, with nothing installed.  No leftovers, no 
old libraries, no cross dependencies, and no OPTIONS set.

there is nothing wrong with the default kde4, nothing wrong with the 
default libxine. There is something amiss in your installation, and 
opening a pr won't help you.

you need interactive help, you need to post more information to the 
freebsd-ports@, or freebsd-x11@ mailing lists.
Maybe email the maintainer of multimedia/libxine, nox@FreeBSD.org


-- 
Michael Scheidell, CTO
 >*| * SECNAP Network Security Corporation
